content:
I think we can avoid the separate initialization step of global locks like this: https://github.com/Darksonn/linux/commits/arch-spin-global/

Thoughts? The idea is to do this:

 #define __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED      { 0 }
+#define __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_TYP  unsigned int
+#define __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_INT  0

and use the new defines on the Rust side.

looks a bit cursed, but I think that is a huge improvement if we can avoid the additional init function. Great idea!
In rust/bindings/lib.rs, I do not like the hardcoded 64 constant... Do you think it's possible to just calculate the required length? If not, then I would add a build_assert! to check that it's fine. Or maybe define the size also on the C side?
The Rust compiler will catch the UB during const evaluation if 64 is too small.
Anyone happens to know what file would be a good place to put a kunit test (on the C side) related to this change?
why do you need a __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_TYP, it should always be an i32, right?
also, I think you need to statically check that __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED is the same value as __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_INT.
See the Todo in the commit message

Boqun Feng said:

why do you need a __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_TYP, it should always be an i32, right?

Ah oh, in SMP=n, spinlock is ZST ;-)
Parisc is another tricky one
You could define the new macro only for the archs that support Rust, right?
True.
But this way it doesn't become something that people adding new archs have to remember to do.
Won't it cause build error if they forget?
It's a bit unclear to me if it'll build error or just fall back to the generic asm header?

Alice Ryhl said:

Parisc is another tricky one

Rust doesn't support this _at all_, so we probably don't need to add it

Alice Ryhl said:

But this way it doesn't become something that people adding new archs have to remember to do.

it will be a compile error when they forget, right?
It's a bit unclear to me if it'll build error or just fall back to the generic asm header?
In general, I thought I could just add some tests for the correct value on the C side, and not bother with making the test arch-specific.
if someone adds a brand-new arch, they would also have this issue, right?
what?
so I don't think it's that bad for people that add rust support for a new arch to have to do add it

Alice Ryhl said:

It's a bit unclear to me if it'll build error or just fall back to the generic asm header?

Arch needs to explicitly select whether it uses "asm-generic" header or not, so there is no "silent inclusion".

Boqun Feng said:

Alice Ryhl said:

It's a bit unclear to me if it'll build error or just fall back to the generic asm header?

Arch needs to explicitly select whether it uses "asm-generic" header or not, so there is no "silent inclusion".

Thanks for the clarification.

Alice Ryhl said:

what?

I was referring to this:

Alice Ryhl said:

But this way it doesn't become something that people adding new archs have to remember to do.

When someone adds support for an arch that isn't yet supported in the kernel, they would have to add this, if they also wanted to enable rust.
so I think it's fine to have people that add rust support for an arch also have to add it
Also, we could make an "architecture checking list" for new arch to implement Rust support, it would be helpful anyway.
But overall, I agree that using a const value to initialize static spinlocks is the right thing to do, thanks for looking into this!
Any advice on where to put a kunit test?
I haven't written any kunit test for C myself, but I find a few _kunit.c file in lib/, so that's probably where it should be put.

but since it can be done static may be just

static_assert(*(__ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_TYP *)(&(arch_spinlock_t __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED)) == __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_INT);

in rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h
Ah nice!
though why not put the test somewhere that runs even if Rust is disabled?
to avoid introducing _TYP and _INT for archs that don't support Rust I think.
We can wrap it in #ifdef __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_TYP
Then probably put it in include/linux/spinlock_types.h.
Ok

Hmm.. but seems a

Boqun Feng said:

but since it can be done static may be just

static_assert(*(__ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_TYP *)(&(arch_spinlock_t __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED)) == __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED_INT);

in rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h

Hmm... looks like this C version "transmute" doesn't work in constant expression...
So it has to be a real kunit test?
Probably, I'm still trying to see if there is an alternative.
Well let me know what you come up with.

so if bindgen can convert a C const to Rust const, we can do a:

const arch_spinlock_t UNLOCKED = __ARCH_SPIN_LOCK_UNLOCKED;

and use that in Rust code?
(it's a bit annoying to work around a bindgen issue with C side changes, sigh)
Yeah if Bingen could handle the struct it would be much easier
